name: Zapruder Film
description: The Zapruder Film is an 8mm silent color motion picture sequence shot by Abraham Zapruder during the assassination of United States President John F. Kennedy on November 22, 1963, in Dallas, Texas. It is arguably the most scrutinized piece of film in history. Conspiracy theories surrounding the assassination often center on interpretations of the film, claiming it provides visual evidence of a second shooter or that the official Warren Commission's conclusions about a single assassin are incorrect. These theories often focus on specific frames of the film, such as frame 313, which shows Kennedy's head snapping violently backward. Proponents argue this backward movement indicates a shot from the front, contradicting the official narrative that Lee Harvey Oswald fired all shots from behind. The film was initially suppressed from public view, adding to the intrigue and fueling speculation. Mainstream scholarly analysis, including forensic analysis and ballistics experts, generally supports the Warren Commission's findings, attributing the head snap to a neuromuscular reaction to the bullet's impact and the subsequent explosive exit wound. While acknowledging the film's disturbing nature and the gaps in the official narrative that allow for interpretation, most mainstream historians and scientists maintain that the Zapruder Film, while valuable, does not definitively prove a conspiracy. Debate persists regarding the authenticity of the film itself, with some conspiracy theorists alleging alterations or manipulation, though no credible evidence supports such claims.
subtitle: Silent Film of Kennedy Assassination

claims.claim: Frame 313 shows Kennedy's head snapping backward, indicating a shot from the front.
claims.evidence: The visible backward head movement in frame 313 of the Zapruder Film.
claims.criticism: Mainstream science attributes the backward head movement to a neuromuscular reaction and the jet effect of the bullet exiting Kennedy's skull.

claims.claim: The 'magic bullet' trajectory is impossible as depicted in the Warren Report, based on the film.
claims.evidence: Analysis of the wounds of Kennedy and Connally in relation to the Zapruder Film and the alleged trajectory of CE 399.
claims.criticism: The Warren Commission's single-bullet theory has been challenged, but subsequent analyses and experiments have attempted to demonstrate its feasibility, though controversy remains.

claims.claim: The Zapruder film has been altered or tampered with to conceal evidence of a conspiracy.
claims.evidence: Allegations of missing frames, inconsistencies in the film's appearance, and claims of photographic analysis revealing alterations.
claims.criticism: Extensive forensic analysis of the original Zapruder Film has not revealed any conclusive evidence of tampering. Claims of alteration are generally based on misinterpretations or flawed analysis.

keyFigures.name: Josiah Thompson
keyFigures.role: Early proponent of the two-gunman theory based on the Zapruder Film

keyFigures.name: Vincent Bugliosi
keyFigures.role: Prominent defender of the Warren Commission's findings

mythologyConnections: The death of a king/hero archetype

timeline.event: John F. Kennedy is assassinated; Zapruder films the event.
Life magazine purchases the rights to the Zapruder Film.
The Zapruder Film is shown on national television for the first time.
The Zapruder Film is declared a national treasure and placed under the care of the National Archives.
